body:
### Problem The vim.cmd API currently only accepts numeric values for the optional `range` parameter when used in "table form", where the command is then passed to vim.api.nvim_cmd For example: ```lua vim.cmd.foldopen({ bang = true, range = { 1, 10 }, mods = { silent = true } }) ``` All other [`:h :range`](https://neovim.io/doc/user/cmdline/#%3Arange) modes like `:.`, `:$`, `:%` or `:*` are unsupported. As are single range specifiers, both numeric and symbolic. This makes something like a `:.,%foldclose!` impractical via the table form of `vim.cmd`, since the equivalent; ```lua vim.cmd.foldclose({ bang = true, range = {'.', '%'} }) ``` is currently invalid. My grasp of C isn't the greatest but I believe the range gets handled here in `nvim_cmd`. https://github.com/neovim/neovim/blob/925e9e87222cba0c48d2a9184fa3e5fcad65d4ba/src/nvim/api/command.c#L569-L577 ### Expected behavior Handling *all* range addressing modes would require a significant amount of additional parsing code in the cmdline parsing. That code already exists *somewhere* in `nvim_parse_cmd` since that handles non-integer range specifiers just fine. I'm guessing that *somewhere* is here via the `parse_cmdline` function. https://github.com/neovim/neovim/blob/925e9e87222cba0c48d2a9184fa3e5fcad65d4ba/src/nvim/api/command.c#L135-L142 Which looks like a fun can of worms in and of itself. But eventually calls down into the `parse_cmd_address` function. https://github.com/neovim/neovim/blob/925e9e87222cba0c48d2a9184fa3e5fcad65d4ba/src/nvim/ex_docmd.c#L2919 Which has handling for the `%` and `*` variants. https://github.com/neovim/neovim/blob/925e9e87222cba0c48d2a9184fa3e5fcad65d4ba/src/nvim/ex_docmd.c#L2938 https://github.com/neovim/neovim/blob/925e9e87222cba0c48d2a9184fa3e5fcad65d4ba/src/nvim/ex_docmd.c#L3002 The handling for the `.`, `'`, `$`, `/`, `?`, `\/`, `\?`, `\&` seems to be handled by the `get_address` function that gets called as part of the `parse_cmd_address` function. So this covers all the range addressing modes listed in [`:h :range`](https://neovim.io/doc/user/cmdline/#%3Arange).

To finally get to the point after falling down that rabbit hole. If we can plumb this up to `nvim_cmd`'s range parsing logic as a parsing step for non-numeric range modes that would allow for greater flexibility when using `vim.cmd({})`, `vim.cmd.foo({})` or `vim.api.nvim_cmd()`. This would also require some additional plumbling to make `vim.cmd`/`nvim_cmd` accept 1 arg addressing modes as well as strings in addition to integers. e.g. ```lua vim.cmd({ cmd = foo, range = {'%'} }) -- % -> 1, vim.fn.line('$') vim.cmd({ cmd = foo, range = '.' }) -- . -> vim.fn.line('.') vim.cmd({ cmd = foo, range = {'.', '$'} }) -- % -> vim.fn.line('.'), vim.fn.line('$') ``` One remaining unknown is how to best handle [`n;m` range addressing](https://neovim.io/doc/user/cmdline/#%3A%3B) which isn't currently being accounted for in `nvim_cmd`'s range handling. The sanest option may just be adding a new type to `vim.api.keyset.cmd` for `m;n` ranges. Maybe something like: ```lua --- @alias range_mode integer|string --- @class vim.api.keyset.cmd_range --- @field lines? (range_mode|[range_mode?, range_mode?])? --- @field semicolon? boolean? ``` `m;n` ranges don't really have any good name in the documentation, maybe *"eager"* could work as a name? Since according to the documentation: > **When separated with `;` the cursor position will be set to that line before interpreting the next line specifier. This doesn't happen for `,`.** I have no idea how or if [*range offsets*](https://neovim.io/doc/user/cmdline/#%3Arange-offset) should be handled, but that *should* come "for free" with supporting string range modes since that is supported by `vim.api.nvim_parse_cmd`/`parse_cmdline` There may also be performance considerations in regards to reusing the `parse_cmdline` codepath in `nvim_cmd`/`vim.cmd`.

I'm hoping my sleep deprived rambling was still coherent enough to follow. - If this has been discussed or reported before I'm sorry that I wasn't able to find that. The closest related issue I was able to find is #18393. But this particular point doesn't seem to have been mentioned there. Working around this problem is possible, but much less ergonomic to use. So supporting non-numeric range specifiers in `vim.cmd` table mode would be a nice quality of life improvement. If the juice is worth the squeeze, I'm not sure though.
title: `vim.cmd` does not accept the full set of Ex command-line range specifiers

type.description: A request, idea, or new functionality
type.name: Enhancement
